zh.Walheim, Württemberg, Deutschland: 德國爾特波格可萊斯 貝希海姆瓦爾海姆
zh.Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfal: 德國海藍-普法爾茲海-拉-可萊斯福盧特
zh.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck於1639年出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲海-拉-可萊斯福盧特。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1671年在他的家鄉享年32歲，埋葬在德國爾特波格可萊斯 貝希海姆瓦爾海姆。

zh-hans.Walheim, Württemberg, Deutschland: 德国爾特波格可萊斯 貝希海姆瓦爾海姆
zh-hans.Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfal: 德国海藍-普法爾茲海-拉-可萊斯福盧特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck于1639年出生在德国海藍-普法爾茲海-拉-可萊斯福盧特。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1671年在他的家乡享年32岁，埋葬在德国爾特波格可萊斯 貝希海姆瓦爾海姆。

nameMeanings.name: Beck
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from northern Middle English bekke ‘stream’ (Old Norse bekkr).

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named Bec, for example Bec Hellouin in Eure, named with Old Norman French bec ‘stream’, from the same Old Norse root as in 1 above.
English: from the Middle English personal name Becke (Old English Becca or Beocca), of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Beckett
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French, Middle English beket ‘small beak’ (a diminutive of Old French bec ‘beak or mouth’), perhaps with reference to someone with a small beak-like mouth or a prominent nose (compare Beck 4), though the Middle English word appears to have had a wide range of applications denoting ‘pointed objects’, including ‘corbel, kind of arrow, kind of bird’, and ‘kind of fish’. This surname is also found in Ireland, especially in Antrim.

English: habitational name from any of the places called Beckett in Berkshire and Devon. The former is named with Old English bēo ‘bee’ + cot ‘cottage, shelter’; the latter has as its first element the Old English personal name Bicca.
Altered form of   Bequette  , a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Beckford
meanings: English: habitational name from a place now in Worcestershire (formerly in Gloucestershire) named Beckford, from the Middle English name Becke (see Beck 3) + Old English ford ‘ford’. This surname is most frequent in Jamaica.
History: The surname Beckford was taken to Jamaica in the 17th century. Colonel Peter Beckford (1643–1710) served as acting governor of Jamaica in 1702.

variants.name: Beckemeyer
meanings: North German: distinguishing name for a tenant farmer living by a stream (see   Beck   +   Meyer  ).

variants.name: Beckham
meanings: English (Norfolk): habitational name from a place in Norfolk named Beckham, from the Middle English name Becke (see Beck 3) + Old English hām ‘homestead’.

variants.name: Becker
meanings: German and Dutch: occupational name for a baker of bread, or brick and tiles, derived from Middle High German and Middle Dutch backen ‘to bake’. The German surname is also found in France (mainly Lorraine and Alsace).
Danish: occupational name for a baker, from bechere ‘baker’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a baker, from German Bäcker (compare 1 and Backer ) or Yiddish beker ‘baker’.

variants.name: Beckius
meanings: Swedish: ornamental name composed of the element beck, a variant of bäck ‘stream’, + the Latin suffix -ius.
German: Latinized (humanistic) form of Beck ‘baker’ or ‘stream’. The surname Beckius is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Becka
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Bečka): nickname or metonymic occupational name from bečka ‘cask’.
German: variant of   Beck   ‘baker’.

variants.name: Becken
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Thor.
North German: variant of   Beck   6.
Norwegian: very rare variant, and an Americanized form, of   Bekken  .

variants.name: Becking
meanings: Dutch and German: farm name, mainly in the border region of East Netherlands (Overijssel) and Germany (North Rhine-Westphalia), indicating its location ‘beside a stream’ (see   Beck   6).

variants.name: Beckley
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so named in Oxfordshire and Sussex, from the Old English byname Becca (see Beck 3) + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.
English: variant of   Bickley  .
Americanized form of South German Bächle (see Baechle ) and of its Swiss German cognate Bächli.

nameMeanings.name: Johan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Older Scottish spelling of Joan , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables.

variants.name: Jens
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jöns
meanings: Mainly Danish: variant of   Johan .

variants.name: Jan
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jon
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Georg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck was born in 1639, in Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1671, in his hometown, at the age of 32, and was buried in Walheim, Besigheim, Württemberg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck est né en 1639, à Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1671, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Walheim, Kreis Besigheim, Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck wurde 1639 in Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1671 im Alter von 32 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Walheim, Kreis Besigheim, Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck è nato il nel 1639 nel Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1671, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolto a Walheim, Kreis Besigheim, Württemberg, Germania.

ja.Walheim, Württemberg, Deutschland: ドイツウuルッテンバーグクレイス ベシグヘイムワルヘイム
ja.Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfal: ドイツルヘインランド-プファルズルヘイン-ラン-クレイスフルuチト
ja.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beckは1639年にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズルヘイン-ラン-クレイスフルuチトで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1671年に故郷で32歳で死去し，ドイツウuルッテンバーグクレイス ベシグヘイムワルヘイムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck(은)는 1639년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 리이-라h-크레이스 프uch트에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1671년 그의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 wu르테버그 크레이스 베시게이임 월하임에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck nasceu em 1639, em Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1671, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em Walheim, Kreis Besigheim, Württemberg, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck родился в 1639 г. в местности фрuчт, раин-лахн-крис, раинланд-пфалз, Германия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1671 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности валхим, крис бесаиим, вuрттембирг, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Johan Georg Beck nació en 1639, en Frücht, Rhein-Lahn-Kreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1671, en su pueblo, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultado en Walheim, Kreis Besigheim, Württemberg, Alemania.
